question_en: I just joined my first job earning Rs 8 lakh per year. My HR is asking me to declare tax-saving investments for FY 2025-26. I'm thinking of starting a Rs 12,500 monthly PPF contribution and paying Rs 15,000 annual life insurance premium. My employer also deducts Rs 28,800 per year towards EPF (my share). Will all of this fit within my 80C limit? How much more room do I have to invest in ELSS or other 80C instruments?
question_hi: मैंने अभी पहली नौकरी शुरू की है, सालाना Rs 8 लाख कमाता हूं। HR ने FY 2025-26 के लिए tax-saving investments declare करने को कहा है। मैं सोच रहा हूं कि Rs 12,500 महीने PPF में डालूं और Rs 15,000 सालाना life insurance premium दूं। Employer EPF में मेरा हिस्सा Rs 28,800 सालाना काट रहा है। क्या यह सब मेरी 80C सीमा में आ जाएगा? ELSS या अन्य 80C instruments में और कितना निवेश कर सकता हूं?
question_hinglish: Maine abhi pehli job join ki hai, saal ka 8 lakh kamata hoon. HR ne FY 2025-26 ke liye tax-saving investments declare karne bola hai. Soch raha hoon ki Rs 12,500 monthly PPF mein daalun aur Rs 15,000 annual life insurance premium doon. Employer EPF mein mera share Rs 28,800 per year kaat raha hai. Kya yeh sab 80C limit mein aa jayega? ELSS ya doosre 80C instruments mein aur kitna invest kar sakta hoon?
answer: Your total 80C investments: PPF = Rs 12,500 x 12 = Rs 1,50,000; Life insurance premium = Rs 15,000; Employee EPF contribution = Rs 28,800. Total = Rs 1,93,800. However, the maximum Section 80C deduction is Rs 1,50,000 per financial year. Since your PPF alone exhausts the full Rs 1.5 lakh limit, your life insurance premium (Rs 15,000) and EPF contribution (Rs 28,800) will NOT give you any additional tax benefit under 80C. You have zero room for ELSS or other 80C instruments. Consider reducing your PPF contribution to Rs 8,850/month (Rs 1,06,200/year) so that PPF + EPF + LIC = Rs 1,50,000 exactly. Alternatively, the Rs 50,000 NPS contribution under Section 80CCD(1B) is a separate deduction above the 80C limit.
question_te: నేను ఇప్పుడే first job join అయ్యాను, సంవత్సరానికి Rs 8 లక్షలు సంపాదిస్తున్నాను. HR FY 2025-26 కి tax-saving investments declare చేయమని చెప్పారు. నెలకు Rs 12,500 PPF contribution, Rs 15,000 annual life insurance premium plan చేస్తున్నాను. Employer EPF లో నా share Rs 28,800 per year cut చేస్తున్నారు. ఇవన్నీ 80C limit లో ఇమడతాయా? ELSS లేదా ఇతర 80C instruments లో ఇంకెంత invest చేయగలను?
question_bn: আমি সবে প্রথম চাকরিতে যোগ দিয়েছি, বছরে Rs 8 লক্ষ আয়। HR FY 2025-26-এর জন্য tax-saving investments declare করতে বলেছে। মাসে Rs 12,500 PPF contribution আর বছরে Rs 15,000 life insurance premium দেওয়ার কথা ভাবছি। Employer EPF-এ আমার share Rs 28,800 per year কাটছে। এসব কি 80C limit-এ ফিট হবে? ELSS বা অন্য 80C instruments-এ আর কত invest করতে পারব?
question_ta: நான் இப்போதுதான் first job join ஆனேன், வருடத்துக்கு Rs 8 லட்சம் சம்பாதிக்கிறேன். HR FY 2025-26-க்கு tax-saving investments declare பண்ண சொல்லிருக்காங்க. மாசம் Rs 12,500 PPF contribution, Rs 15,000 annual life insurance premium plan பண்றேன். Employer EPF-ல என் share Rs 28,800 per year cut பண்றாங்க. இதெல்லாம் 80C limit-ல fit ஆகுமா? ELSS அல்லது வேற 80C instruments-ல இன்னும் எவ்வளவு invest பண்ண முடியும்?

question_en: I earn Rs 12 lakh per year. Should I choose the old tax regime or the new tax regime for FY 2025-26? What would be my tax liability under each?
question_hi: मेरी सालाना आय 12 लाख रुपये है। क्या मुझे FY 2025-26 के लिए पुरानी कर व्यवस्था चुननी चाहिए या नई? दोनों में मेरा कर कितना होगा?
question_hinglish: Meri salary 12 lakh per year hai. Old regime ya new regime, kaunsa better hai FY 2025-26 ke liye? Dono mein kitna tax lagega?
answer: Under the new regime (default, as revised by Budget 2025): Rs 0-4 lakh is nil, Rs 4-8 lakh at 5%, Rs 8-12 lakh at 10%, Rs 12-16 lakh at 15%, Rs 16-20 lakh at 20%, Rs 20-24 lakh at 25%, above Rs 24 lakh at 30%. With the tax rebate increased to Rs 60,000, income up to Rs 12 lakh is effectively tax-free. For salaried individuals, standard deduction of Rs 75,000 means income up to Rs 12.75 lakh is tax-free. Under old regime: depends on deductions claimed (80C, HRA, etc.). For Rs 12 lakh income, the new regime is almost certainly better unless you have very high deductions.
source: Finance Act 2025, Section 115BAC (revised slabs effective FY 2025-26)
question_te: నా annual income 12 లక్షలు. FY 2025-26 కి old tax regime ఎంచుకోవాలా లేదా new regime ఎంచుకోవాలా? రెండింటిలో నా tax ఎంత అవుతుంది?
question_bn: আমার বার্ষিক আয় 12 লক্ষ টাকা। FY 2025-26-এর জন্য old tax regime নেব না new regime? দুটোতে আমার tax কত হবে?
question_ta: என் வருடாந்திர வருமானம் 12 லட்சம். FY 2025-26-க்கு old tax regime தேர்வு செய்யணுமா இல்ல new regime-ஆ? ரெண்டுலயும் என் tax எவ்வளவு வரும்?

question_en: I sold equity shares after holding for 14 months and made a profit of Rs 2 lakh. What is my tax liability on this capital gain for FY 2025-26?
question_hi: मैंने 14 महीने रखने के बाद इक्विटी शेयर बेचे और 2 लाख रुपये का मुनाफा कमाया। FY 2025-26 में इस पूंजीगत लाभ पर मेरा कर कितना होगा?
question_hinglish: Maine shares 14 months hold karke sell kiye, 2 lakh ka profit hua. FY 2025-26 mein isme kitna tax lagega?
answer: Since shares were held for more than 12 months, this is Long Term Capital Gain (LTCG). Under Section 112A (as amended by Budget 2024), LTCG on listed equity above Rs 1.25 lakh is taxed at 12.5%. Tax = 12.5% of (Rs 2,00,000 - Rs 1,25,000) = 12.5% of Rs 75,000 = Rs 9,375 plus 4% cess = Rs 9,750.
source: Section 112A, Income Tax Act (as amended by Finance Act 2024)
question_te: నేను 14 months hold చేసిన equity shares అమ్మాను, 2 లక్షల profit వచ్చింది. FY 2025-26 లో ఈ capital gain మీద నా tax ఎంత?
question_bn: আমি 14 মাস ধরে রাখা equity shares বিক্রি করে 2 লক্ষ টাকা profit করেছি। FY 2025-26-তে এই capital gain-এ আমার tax কত হবে?
question_ta: நான் 14 months hold பண்ணின equity shares விற்றேன், 2 லட்சம் profit வந்தது. FY 2025-26-ல இந்த capital gain-க்கு என் tax எவ்வளவு?

question_en: I sold mutual fund units (equity-oriented) after holding for 8 months and made a profit of Rs 50,000. What is the tax treatment for FY 2025-26?
question_hi: मैंने इक्विटी-ओरिएंटेड म्यूचुअल फंड यूनिट्स 8 महीने बाद बेचे और 50,000 रुपये का मुनाफा कमाया। FY 2025-26 में इसका कर उपचार क्या है?
question_hinglish: Maine equity mutual fund units 8 months baad sell kiye, 50 hazaar ka profit hua. Tax kaise lagega FY 2025-26 mein?
answer: Since equity-oriented mutual fund units were held for less than 12 months, this is Short Term Capital Gain (STCG) under Section 111A. STCG tax rate is 20% (revised from 15% in Budget 2024). Tax = 20% of Rs 50,000 = Rs 10,000 plus 4% cess = Rs 10,400.
source: Section 111A, Income Tax Act (as amended by Finance Act 2024)
question_te: నేను equity-oriented mutual fund units 8 months తర్వాత sell చేశాను, Rs 50,000 profit వచ్చింది. FY 2025-26 లో tax treatment ఏమిటి?
question_bn: আমি equity-oriented mutual fund units 8 মাস পরে বিক্রি করেছি, Rs 50,000 profit হয়েছে। FY 2025-26-তে এর tax treatment কী?
question_ta: நான் equity-oriented mutual fund units-ஐ 8 months-க்குப் பிறகு sell பண்ணினேன், Rs 50,000 profit வந்தது. FY 2025-26-ல tax treatment என்ன?

question_en: I live in Mumbai, pay rent of Rs 25,000 per month, my basic salary is Rs 40,000 per month, and I receive HRA of Rs 20,000 per month. How much HRA exemption can I claim under the old regime?
question_hi: मैं मुंबई में रहता हूं, 25,000 रुपये प्रति माह किराया देता हूं, मेरी बेसिक सैलरी 40,000 रुपये प्रति माह है, और HRA 20,000 रुपये प्रति माह मिलता है। पुरानी व्यवस्था में मुझे कितनी HRA छूट मिलेगी?
question_hinglish: Main Mumbai mein rehta hoon, 25,000 rent deta hoon per month, basic salary 40,000 hai aur HRA 20,000 milta hai. Old regime mein HRA exemption kitna milega?
answer: HRA exemption is the minimum of: (1) Actual HRA received = Rs 20,000/month = Rs 2,40,000/year; (2) 50% of basic salary (for metro cities) = Rs 20,000/month = Rs 2,40,000/year; (3) Rent paid minus 10% of basic salary = Rs 25,000 - Rs 4,000 = Rs 21,000/month = Rs 2,52,000/year. Minimum of the three = Rs 2,40,000 per year. HRA exemption = Rs 2,40,000.
source: Section 10(13A) read with Rule 2A of Income Tax Rules
question_te: నేను Mumbai లో ఉంటాను, నెలకు Rs 25,000 rent కడుతున్నాను, basic salary Rs 40,000, HRA Rs 20,000 వస్తుంది. Old regime లో నాకు ఎంత HRA exemption వస్తుంది?
question_bn: আমি Mumbai-তে থাকি, মাসে Rs 25,000 rent দিই, basic salary Rs 40,000, HRA Rs 20,000 পাই। Old regime-এ আমার HRA exemption কত হবে?
question_ta: நான் Mumbai-ல இருக்கேன், மாசம் Rs 25,000 rent கட்டுறேன், basic salary Rs 40,000, HRA Rs 20,000 வருது. Old regime-ல எனக்கு எவ்வளவு HRA exemption கிடைக்கும்?

question_en: I am 35 years old. I pay Rs 30,000 for my health insurance premium and Rs 50,000 for my parents' health insurance (both parents are senior citizens above 60). What is my total deduction under Section 80D?
question_hi: मेरी उम्र 35 साल है। मैं अपने स्वास्थ्य बीमा प्रीमियम के लिए 30,000 रुपये और अपने माता-पिता (दोनों 60 वर्ष से ऊपर वरिष्ठ नागरिक) के स्वास्थ्य बीमा के लिए 50,000 रुपये भुगतान करता हूं। धारा 80D के तहत मेरी कुल कटौती कितनी होगी?
question_hinglish: Meri age 35 hai. Mera health insurance premium 30,000 hai aur parents ka 50,000 (dono senior citizens hain 60 se upar). Section 80D mein total kitna deduction milega?
answer: For self (below 60): Maximum deduction is Rs 25,000. Since you paid Rs 30,000, you can claim Rs 25,000. For parents (senior citizens above 60): Maximum deduction is Rs 50,000. Since you paid Rs 50,000, you can claim Rs 50,000. Total Section 80D deduction = Rs 25,000 + Rs 50,000 = Rs 75,000.
question_te: నా వయసు 35 ఏళ్లు. నా health insurance premium Rs 30,000, parents health insurance Rs 50,000 (ఇద్దరూ 60 ఏళ్ల పైన senior citizens). Section 80D కింద total deduction ఎంత వస్తుంది?
question_bn: আমার বয়স 35 বছর। আমার health insurance premium Rs 30,000 আর parents-এর Rs 50,000 (দুজনেই 60-এর ওপরে senior citizens)। Section 80D-তে total deduction কত পাব?
question_ta: எனக்கு வயசு 35. என் health insurance premium Rs 30,000, parents-ோட health insurance Rs 50,000 (இருவரும் 60 வயசுக்கு மேல senior citizens). Section 80D-ல total deduction எவ்வளவு கிடைக்கும்?

question_en: My employer deducts TDS but I also have income from freelancing of Rs 3 lakh. Do I need to file ITR? Can I face penalty if I don't?
question_hi: मेरा नियोक्ता TDS काटता है लेकिन मेरी फ्रीलांसिंग से भी 3 लाख रुपये की आय है। क्या मुझे ITR दाखिल करना होगा? अगर नहीं करूं तो जुर्माना हो सकता है?
question_hinglish: Mera employer TDS kaat ta hai but freelancing se bhi 3 lakh ki income hai. Kya ITR file karna zaroori hai? Nahi kiya toh penalty lagegi?
answer: Yes, you must file ITR if your total income exceeds the basic exemption limit (Rs 3 lakh under new regime, Rs 2.5 lakh under old regime). Since you have salary income plus Rs 3 lakh freelancing income, your total income very likely exceeds the limit. Freelancing income is taxable under 'Profits and Gains from Business or Profession'. Late filing penalty under Section 234F is Rs 5,000 if filed after due date but before December 31, and Rs 10,000 after that. For total income below Rs 5 lakh, penalty is limited to Rs 1,000.
question_te: నా employer TDS cut చేస్తాడు కానీ freelancing నుండి కూడా Rs 3 లక్షల income ఉంది. నేను ITR file చేయాలా? చేయకపోతే penalty వస్తుందా?
question_bn: আমার employer TDS কাটে কিন্তু freelancing থেকেও Rs 3 লক্ষ income আছে। আমাকে কি ITR file করতে হবে? না করলে penalty হবে?
question_ta: என் employer TDS பிடிக்கிறார் ஆனா freelancing-லிருந்தும் Rs 3 லட்சம் income இருக்கு. நான் ITR file பண்ணணுமா? பண்ணலன்னா penalty வருமா?

question_en: I am an NRI working in the US. I have a savings account in India earning Rs 2 lakh interest per year. Is this taxable in India? What rate?
question_hi: मैं अमेरिका में काम करने वाला NRI हूं। मेरे भारत के बचत खाते में सालाना 2 लाख रुपये ब्याज आता है। क्या यह भारत में कर योग्य है? किस दर से?
question_hinglish: Main NRI hoon US mein kaam karta hoon. India mein savings account se 2 lakh interest aata hai per year. Kya India mein tax lagega? Kitna?
answer: Yes, interest income earned in India is taxable in India for NRIs. Interest from savings account in India is taxed at the applicable slab rates for NRIs. NRIs can benefit from the India-US Double Taxation Avoidance Agreement (DTAA) to claim credit for taxes paid in India against their US tax liability. TDS at 30% is deducted on NRI savings account interest by the bank. NRIs can claim Section 80TTA deduction of Rs 10,000 on savings account interest.
question_te: నేను US లో పని చేస్తున్న NRI ని. India లో savings account నుండి సంవత్సరానికి Rs 2 లక్షల interest వస్తోంది. ఇది India లో taxable ఏనా? ఎంత rate?
question_bn: আমি US-তে কাজ করা NRI। India-তে savings account থেকে বছরে Rs 2 লক্ষ interest আসে। এটা কি India-তে taxable? কত rate-এ?
question_ta: நான் US-ல வேலை பார்க்கிற NRI. India-ல savings account-லிருந்து வருடத்துக்கு Rs 2 லட்சம் interest வருது. இது India-ல taxable-ஆ? எவ்வளவு rate?

question_en: My CTC is Rs 15 lakh. My CA says I should switch to the new regime since I don't have many deductions. But I contribute Rs 50,000 to NPS myself and my employer puts 14% of my basic salary (Rs 6 lakh basic) into NPS under 80CCD(2). I also pay Rs 20,000 health insurance premium. Will I lose the NPS benefit if I switch to the new regime? What deductions can I still claim under the new regime for FY 2025-26?
question_hi: मेरा CTC Rs 15 लाख है। CA कह रहे हैं नई व्यवस्था में जाना चाहिए क्योंकि मेरे पास ज्यादा deductions नहीं हैं। लेकिन मैं खुद Rs 50,000 NPS में डालता हूं और employer मेरी basic salary (Rs 6 लाख basic) का 14% NPS में 80CCD(2) के तहत देता है। Health insurance premium भी Rs 20,000 देता हूं। क्या new regime में NPS का फायदा खो जाएगा? FY 2025-26 में new regime में कौन-कौन सी deductions मिल सकती हैं?
question_hinglish: Mera CTC 15 lakh hai. CA bol raha hai new regime mein chale jao kyunki zyada deductions nahi hain. Lekin main khud 50,000 NPS mein daalta hoon aur employer basic salary (6 lakh basic) ka 14% NPS mein 80CCD(2) ke under deta hai. Health insurance premium bhi 20,000 deta hoon. Kya new regime mein NPS ka benefit chala jayega? FY 2025-26 mein new regime mein kaunsi deductions mil sakti hain?
answer: Under the new tax regime (Section 115BAC, as revised by Budget 2025), here is what happens to your specific deductions: (1) Your own NPS contribution of Rs 50,000 under Section 80CCD(1B): NOT available under new regime - you lose this. (2) Employer's NPS contribution under 80CCD(2) of 14% of Rs 6 lakh = Rs 84,000: This IS available under new regime - you keep this benefit. (3) Health insurance premium of Rs 20,000 under 80D: NOT available under new regime - you lose this. (4) Standard deduction of Rs 75,000 for salaried: Available under new regime. (5) Family pension deduction up to Rs 25,000: Available if applicable. (6) Transport allowance for disabled: Available if applicable. Budget 2025 raised the nil slab to Rs 4 lakh and introduced a rebate making income up to Rs 12 lakh effectively tax-free (Rs 12.75 lakh for salaried with standard deduction). So you lose Rs 70,000 in deductions (50K NPS self + 20K health insurance) but gain from lower slab rates. With Rs 15 lakh CTC, the new regime is likely better for you since your claimable old-regime deductions are modest.
question_te: నా CTC Rs 15 లక్షలు. CA new regime కి switch అవ్వమని చెప్తున్నారు ఎందుకంటే నాకు ఎక్కువ deductions లేవు. కానీ నేను NPS లో Rs 50,000 వేస్తున్నాను, employer basic salary (Rs 6 లక్షల basic) లో 14% NPS లో 80CCD(2) కింద వేస్తున్నారు. Health insurance premium Rs 20,000 కూడా కడుతున్నాను. New regime కి switch అయితే NPS benefit పోతుందా? FY 2025-26 లో new regime లో ఏ deductions claim చేయగలను?
question_bn: আমার CTC Rs 15 লক্ষ। CA বলছে new regime-এ যেতে কারণ আমার বেশি deductions নেই। কিন্তু আমি নিজে Rs 50,000 NPS-এ দিই আর employer basic salary-র (Rs 6 লক্ষ basic) 14% NPS-এ 80CCD(2)-তে দেয়। Health insurance premium-ও Rs 20,000 দিই। New regime-এ গেলে NPS benefit হারাব? FY 2025-26-তে new regime-এ কোন deductions claim করতে পারব?
question_ta: என் CTC Rs 15 லட்சம். CA new regime-க்கு switch பண்ணச் சொல்றார் ஏன்னா எனக்கு அதிகம் deductions இல்ல. ஆனா நான் Rs 50,000 NPS-ல போடுறேன், employer basic salary-ன் (Rs 6 லட்சம் basic) 14% NPS-ல 80CCD(2) கீழ் போடுறார். Health insurance premium-ம் Rs 20,000 கட்டுறேன். New regime-க்கு switch பண்ணா NPS benefit போயிடுமா? FY 2025-26-ல new regime-ல என்னென்ன deductions claim பண்ண முடியும்?

question_en: I made Rs 5 lakh profit trading cryptocurrency in India. How is this taxed in FY 2025-26?
question_hi: मैंने भारत में क्रिप्टोकरेंसी ट्रेडिंग से 5 लाख रुपये का मुनाफा कमाया। FY 2025-26 में इस पर कैसे कर लगेगा?
question_hinglish: Maine crypto trading se 5 lakh ka profit kiya India mein. FY 2025-26 mein isme kitna tax lagega?
answer: Under Section 115BBH (introduced in Budget 2022), all income from transfer of Virtual Digital Assets (VDAs including cryptocurrency) is taxed at a flat rate of 30% plus 4% cess, regardless of your income slab. No deductions or exemptions are allowed except the cost of acquisition. You cannot set off losses from crypto against any other income. Additionally, 1% TDS under Section 194S applies on transactions above Rs 10,000 (Rs 50,000 for specified persons). Tax = 30% of Rs 5,00,000 = Rs 1,50,000 plus 4% cess = Rs 1,56,000.
source: Sections 115BBH, 194S, Income Tax Act (inserted by Finance Act 2022)
question_te: India లో cryptocurrency trading ద్వారా Rs 5 లక్షల profit వచ్చింది. FY 2025-26 లో దీని మీద tax ఎలా వస్తుంది?
question_bn: India-তে cryptocurrency trading থেকে Rs 5 লক্ষ profit হয়েছে। FY 2025-26-তে এটার ওপর tax কীভাবে লাগবে?
question_ta: India-ல cryptocurrency trading-ல Rs 5 லட்சம் profit வந்தது. FY 2025-26-ல இதுக்கு tax எப்படி வரும்?

question_en: My friend gifted me Rs 75,000 in cash on my birthday. Is this taxable under Indian income tax law for FY 2025-26?
question_hi: मेरे दोस्त ने मुझे जन्मदिन पर 75,000 रुपये नकद उपहार दिया। क्या यह भारतीय आयकर कानून के तहत FY 2025-26 में कर योग्य है?
question_hinglish: Mere friend ne mujhe birthday pe 75,000 cash gift diya. Kya yeh FY 2025-26 mein taxable hai Indian income tax mein?
question_te: నా friend నాకు birthday కి Rs 75,000 cash gift ఇచ్చాడు. FY 2025-26 లో Indian income tax law కింద ఇది taxable ఏనా?
question_bn: আমার বন্ধু আমাকে জন্মদিনে Rs 75,000 cash gift দিয়েছে। FY 2025-26-তে Indian income tax law-এ এটা কি taxable?
question_ta: என் friend எனக்கு birthday-க்கு Rs 75,000 cash gift கொடுத்தார். FY 2025-26-ல Indian income tax law-ல இது taxable-ஆ?
answer: Yes, this is taxable. Under Section 56(2)(x), any sum of money received without consideration from a non-relative exceeding Rs 50,000 in aggregate during a financial year is fully taxable as 'Income from Other Sources'. Since your friend is not a 'relative' as defined under the Act, the entire Rs 75,000 (not just the amount exceeding Rs 50,000) becomes taxable. If the gift were from a relative (spouse, sibling, parent, etc.) or received on the occasion of marriage, it would be fully exempt regardless of amount.

question_en: I earn Rs 8 lakh from agriculture and Rs 6 lakh from salary. Is my agricultural income fully exempt? How is my total income taxed for FY 2025-26?
question_hi: मेरी कृषि आय 8 लाख रुपये और वेतन आय 6 लाख रुपये है। क्या मेरी कृषि आय पूरी तरह कर-मुक्त है? FY 2025-26 में मेरी कुल आय पर कर कैसे लगेगा?
question_hinglish: Meri agriculture se 8 lakh aur salary se 6 lakh ki income hai. Kya agricultural income fully exempt hai? FY 2025-26 mein total income pe tax kaise lagega?
question_te: నా agricultural income Rs 8 లక్షలు, salary Rs 6 లక్షలు. నా agricultural income పూర్తిగా exempt ఏనా? FY 2025-26 లో total income మీద tax ఎలా వస్తుంది?
question_bn: আমার agricultural income Rs 8 লক্ষ আর salary Rs 6 লক্ষ। আমার agricultural income কি সম্পূর্ণ exempt? FY 2025-26-তে total income-এ tax কীভাবে হবে?
question_ta: என் agricultural income Rs 8 லட்சம், salary Rs 6 லட்சம். என் agricultural income முழுவதும் exempt-ஆ? FY 2025-26-ல total income-க்கு tax எப்படி வரும்?
answer: Agricultural income is exempt under Section 10(1), but it is used for rate purposes (partial integration method) under the old regime if agricultural income exceeds Rs 5,000 AND total non-agricultural income exceeds the basic exemption limit. Under the old regime: (1) Tax is computed on aggregate income (Rs 8L + Rs 6L = Rs 14L); (2) Tax is computed on agricultural income plus basic exemption (Rs 8L + Rs 2.5L = Rs 10.5L); (3) Difference is your tax liability. Under the new regime (Section 115BAC), agricultural income is NOT clubbed for rate purposes, so only Rs 6 lakh salary is taxed at new regime slab rates, and with Budget 2025 rebate, tax on Rs 6 lakh is nil.
source: Section 10(1), Section 2(1A), Income Tax Act; Rule 8 of IT Rules

question_en: I have a tax liability of Rs 1.5 lakh for FY 2025-26 after TDS. What are the advance tax payment deadlines, and what happens if I miss them?
question_hi: TDS के बाद FY 2025-26 के लिए मेरी कर देनदारी 1.5 लाख रुपये है। अग्रिम कर भुगतान की समय सीमा क्या है, और अगर मैं चूक गया तो क्या होगा?
question_hinglish: TDS ke baad FY 2025-26 mein meri tax liability 1.5 lakh hai. Advance tax payment ki deadlines kya hain, aur miss kiya toh kya hoga?
question_te: TDS తర్వాత FY 2025-26 కి నా tax liability Rs 1.5 లక్షలు. Advance tax payment deadlines ఏమిటి, miss చేస్తే ఏమవుతుంది?
question_bn: TDS-এর পরে FY 2025-26-তে আমার tax liability Rs 1.5 লক্ষ। Advance tax payment-এর deadlines কী কী, আর miss করলে কী হবে?
question_ta: TDS-க்குப் பிறகு FY 2025-26-ல என் tax liability Rs 1.5 லட்சம். Advance tax payment deadlines என்ன, miss பண்ணா என்ன ஆகும்?
answer: Advance tax is mandatory if your tax liability after TDS exceeds Rs 10,000 in a financial year. The deadlines for FY 2025-26 are: (1) By June 15 - at least 15% of advance tax; (2) By September 15 - at least 45% (cumulative); (3) By December 15 - at least 75% (cumulative); (4) By March 15 - 100%. For missing deadlines, interest under Section 234C is charged at 1% per month on the shortfall for 3 months. Additionally, Section 234B applies at 1% per month if total advance tax paid is less than 90% of assessed tax. For Rs 1.5 lakh liability: pay Rs 22,500 by June 15, Rs 67,500 by Sep 15, Rs 1,12,500 by Dec 15, and Rs 1,50,000 by March 15.

question_en: I took an education loan of Rs 15 lakh for my MBA and paid Rs 1.8 lakh as interest in FY 2025-26. Can I claim a deduction under Section 80E? Is there any limit?
question_hi: मैंने MBA के लिए 15 लाख रुपये का शिक्षा ऋण लिया और FY 2025-26 में 1.8 लाख रुपये ब्याज चुकाया। क्या मैं धारा 80E के तहत कटौती ले सकता हूं? क्या कोई सीमा है?
question_hinglish: Maine MBA ke liye 15 lakh ka education loan liya aur FY 2025-26 mein 1.8 lakh interest pay kiya. Kya Section 80E mein deduction mil sakta hai? Koi limit hai?
question_te: నేను MBA కోసం Rs 15 లక్షల education loan తీసుకున్నాను, FY 2025-26 లో Rs 1.8 లక్షల interest చెల్లించాను. Section 80E కింద deduction claim చేయగలనా? Limit ఏదైనా ఉందా?
question_bn: আমি MBA-র জন্য Rs 15 লক্ষ education loan নিয়েছি আর FY 2025-26-তে Rs 1.8 লক্ষ interest দিয়েছি। Section 80E-তে deduction পাব কি? কোনো limit আছে?
question_ta: நான் MBA-க்கு Rs 15 லட்சம் education loan எடுத்தேன், FY 2025-26-ல Rs 1.8 லட்சம் interest கட்டினேன். Section 80E-ல deduction claim பண்ண முடியுமா? Limit ஏதாவது இருக்கா?
answer: Yes, the entire Rs 1.8 lakh interest paid on the education loan is deductible under Section 80E. There is NO upper limit on the deduction amount - you can claim the entire interest paid. However, important conditions: (1) The loan must be from a financial institution or approved charitable institution (not from relatives or friends); (2) Only INTEREST is deductible, not principal repayment; (3) The deduction is available for 8 years starting from the year you begin repaying, or until the interest is fully paid, whichever is earlier; (4) The loan must be for higher education of self, spouse, or children; (5) This deduction is available only under the old tax regime, NOT under the new regime.

question_en: I donated Rs 50,000 to PM CARES Fund and Rs 30,000 to a local NGO approved under Section 80G. What is my total deduction for FY 2025-26?
question_hi: मैंने PM CARES Fund में 50,000 रुपये और Section 80G के तहत मान्यता प्राप्त एक स्थानीय NGO में 30,000 रुपये दान दिए। FY 2025-26 के लिए मेरी कुल कटौती कितनी होगी?
question_hinglish: Maine PM CARES Fund mein 50,000 aur ek local approved NGO mein 30,000 donate kiye. FY 2025-26 mein total deduction kitna milega?
question_te: నేను PM CARES Fund కి Rs 50,000, Section 80G approved local NGO కి Rs 30,000 donate చేశాను. FY 2025-26 లో నా total deduction ఎంత?
question_bn: আমি PM CARES Fund-এ Rs 50,000 আর Section 80G approved একটি local NGO-তে Rs 30,000 donate করেছি। FY 2025-26-তে আমার total deduction কত?
question_ta: நான் PM CARES Fund-க்கு Rs 50,000, Section 80G approved local NGO-க்கு Rs 30,000 donate பண்ணினேன். FY 2025-26-ல என் total deduction எவ்வளவு?
answer: Donations under Section 80G fall into different categories: (1) PM CARES Fund qualifies for 100% deduction without any qualifying limit. So deduction = Rs 50,000. (2) Most local NGOs approved under 80G qualify for 50% deduction subject to the qualifying limit (10% of adjusted gross total income). Assuming your income is high enough, deduction = 50% of Rs 30,000 = Rs 15,000. Total deduction = Rs 50,000 + Rs 15,000 = Rs 65,000. This deduction is available only under the old tax regime. Cash donations above Rs 2,000 are not eligible - use bank transfer, cheque, or online payment.

question_en: I own 3 houses. I live in one, the second is vacant, and the third is rented out for Rs 30,000/month. How is income from house property calculated for FY 2025-26?
question_hi: मेरे पास 3 मकान हैं। एक में मैं रहता हूं, दूसरा खाली है, तीसरा 30,000 रुपये प्रति माह किराये पर दिया है। FY 2025-26 में मकान संपत्ति से आय की गणना कैसे होगी?
question_hinglish: Mere paas 3 ghar hain. Ek mein khud rehta hoon, doosra khali hai, teesra 30,000/month pe rent pe diya hai. FY 2025-26 mein house property income kaise calculate hogi?
question_te: నాకు 3 houses ఉన్నాయి. ఒకటిలో నేను ఉంటున్నాను, రెండోది vacant, మూడోది Rs 30,000/month కి rent కి ఇచ్చాను. FY 2025-26 లో house property income ఎలా calculate అవుతుంది?
question_bn: আমার 3টি বাড়ি আছে। একটিতে থাকি, দ্বিতীয়টি খালি, তৃতীয়টি Rs 30,000/মাসে ভাড়া দিয়েছি। FY 2025-26-তে house property income কীভাবে calculate হবে?
question_ta: எனக்கு 3 houses இருக்கு. ஒன்னுல நான் இருக்கேன், ரெண்டாவது காலி, மூணாவது Rs 30,000/month-க்கு rent-க்கு கொடுத்திருக்கேன். FY 2025-26-ல house property income எப்படி calculate ஆகும்?
answer: From Budget 2019 onwards (applicable FY 2025-26), you can treat up to 2 houses as self-occupied properties (SOP) with nil annual value. (1) House 1 (self-occupied): Annual value = Nil; (2) House 2 (vacant, designated as second SOP): Annual value = Nil; (3) House 3 (rented): Gross Annual Value = Rs 3,60,000 (Rs 30,000 x 12). Less: Municipal taxes paid (assume nil). Net Annual Value = Rs 3,60,000. Less: 30% standard deduction under Section 24(a) = Rs 1,08,000. Net taxable income from house property = Rs 2,52,000. Additionally, home loan interest under Section 24(b) is deductible: up to Rs 2 lakh per SOP and actual interest for let-out property.
source: Sections 22, 23, 24, Income Tax Act (as amended by Finance Act 2019)

question_en: I have a long-term capital loss of Rs 1.5 lakh from selling equity shares and short-term capital gains of Rs 80,000 from debt mutual funds. Can I set off the losses? How does carry forward work?
question_hi: इक्विटी शेयर बेचने से मुझे 1.5 लाख रुपये का दीर्घकालिक पूंजी हानि हुआ और डेट म्यूचुअल फंड से 80,000 रुपये का अल्पकालिक पूंजी लाभ हुआ। क्या मैं नुकसान की भरपाई कर सकता हूं? आगे ले जाना कैसे काम करता है?
question_hinglish: Equity shares bechne se 1.5 lakh ka long-term capital loss hua aur debt mutual funds se 80,000 ka short-term capital gain hua. Kya loss set off kar sakta hoon? Carry forward kaise hota hai?
question_te: Equity shares అమ్మగా Rs 1.5 లక్షల long-term capital loss వచ్చింది, debt mutual funds నుండి Rs 80,000 short-term capital gains వచ్చింది. Losses set off చేయగలనా? Carry forward ఎలా పని చేస్తుంది?
question_bn: Equity shares বিক্রি করে Rs 1.5 লক্ষ long-term capital loss হয়েছে আর debt mutual funds থেকে Rs 80,000 short-term capital gain হয়েছে। Loss set off করতে পারব? Carry forward কীভাবে কাজ করে?
question_ta: Equity shares விற்று Rs 1.5 லட்சம் long-term capital loss வந்தது, debt mutual funds-லிருந்து Rs 80,000 short-term capital gains வந்தது. Losses set off பண்ண முடியுமா? Carry forward எப்படி work ஆகும்?
answer: Key rules for capital loss set-off: (1) Long-term capital loss (LTCL) can ONLY be set off against long-term capital gains (LTCG). It CANNOT be set off against short-term capital gains (STCG). So your Rs 1.5 lakh LTCL from equity CANNOT be set off against Rs 80,000 STCG from debt funds. (2) Short-term capital loss can be set off against both STCG and LTCG. (3) Capital losses cannot be set off against any other head of income (salary, house property, etc.). (4) Unabsorbed LTCL of Rs 1.5 lakh can be carried forward for up to 8 assessment years and set off against future LTCG only. (5) You must file ITR before the due date to carry forward the loss. Your STCG of Rs 80,000 from debt funds is taxable at slab rates.

question_en: I am an Indian resident and earned Rs 5 lakh in dividend income from US stocks. I already paid 25% tax in the US on this. Do I have to pay tax again in India? How does DTAA help?
question_hi: मैं भारतीय निवासी हूं और US स्टॉक्स से 5 लाख रुपये का लाभांश आय अर्जित किया। इस पर US में पहले ही 25% कर चुका दिया है। क्या मुझे भारत में फिर से कर देना होगा? DTAA कैसे मदद करता है?
question_hinglish: Main Indian resident hoon aur US stocks se 5 lakh dividend income earn kiya. US mein already 25% tax pay kar chuka hoon. Kya India mein dobara tax dena hoga? DTAA kaise help karta hai?
question_te: నేను Indian resident ని, US stocks నుండి Rs 5 లక్షల dividend income earn చేశాను. US లో already 25% tax pay చేశాను. India లో మళ్ళీ tax pay చేయాలా? DTAA ఎలా help చేస్తుంది?
question_bn: আমি Indian resident, US stocks থেকে Rs 5 লক্ষ dividend income earned করেছি। US-তে already 25% tax দিয়েছি। India-তে আবার tax দিতে হবে? DTAA কীভাবে help করে?
question_ta: நான் Indian resident, US stocks-லிருந்து Rs 5 லட்சம் dividend income earn பண்ணினேன். US-ல already 25% tax கட்டிட்டேன். India-ல மறுபடியும் tax கட்டணுமா? DTAA எப்படி help பண்ணும்?
answer: As an Indian resident, your global income is taxable in India under Section 5(1). The Rs 5 lakh US dividend must be included in your Indian taxable income under 'Income from Other Sources'. However, under the India-US DTAA (Double Taxation Avoidance Agreement), you can claim relief. The DTAA allows a maximum withholding rate of 25% on dividends in the US. In India, you can claim Foreign Tax Credit (FTC) under Section 90/91 for the US tax paid. You must file Form 67 before filing your ITR to claim FTC. The credit is limited to the lower of: (a) tax paid in the US, or (b) Indian tax attributable to that foreign income. So if your Indian slab rate on that income is 30%, you pay 30% in India but get credit for the 25% paid in the US, effectively paying only 5% additional in India. If your Indian rate is lower than 25%, you can only claim credit up to the Indian rate.
source: Sections 5(1), 90, 91, Income Tax Act; India-US DTAA; Rule 128 (Form 67)

question_en: I am a freelance consultant with annual gross receipts of Rs 40 lakh. Can I opt for presumptive taxation under Section 44ADA for FY 2025-26? What would be my presumptive income?
question_hi: मैं एक फ्रीलांस कंसल्टेंट हूं और मेरी वार्षिक सकल प्राप्तियां 40 लाख रुपये हैं। क्या मैं FY 2025-26 के लिए धारा 44ADA के तहत अनुमानित कराधान का विकल्प चुन सकता हूं? मेरी अनुमानित आय कितनी होगी?
question_hinglish: Main freelance consultant hoon, annual gross receipts 40 lakh hain. Kya FY 2025-26 mein Section 44ADA ke under presumptive taxation le sakta hoon? Meri presumptive income kitni hogi?
question_te: నేను freelance consultant ని, annual gross receipts Rs 40 లక్షలు. FY 2025-26 కి Section 44ADA కింద presumptive taxation opt చేయగలనా? నా presumptive income ఎంత అవుతుంది?
question_bn: আমি freelance consultant, annual gross receipts Rs 40 লক্ষ। FY 2025-26-তে Section 44ADA-র under presumptive taxation নিতে পারব? আমার presumptive income কত হবে?
question_ta: நான் freelance consultant, annual gross receipts Rs 40 லட்சம். FY 2025-26-ல Section 44ADA-ல presumptive taxation opt பண்ண முடியுமா? என் presumptive income எவ்வளவு ஆகும்?
answer: Yes, you can opt for presumptive taxation under Section 44ADA. For professionals, the gross receipts limit was raised to Rs 75 lakh (from Rs 50 lakh) effective from FY 2023-24, provided cash receipts do not exceed 5% of total gross receipts. Since your receipts of Rs 40 lakh are within the limit, you qualify. Under 44ADA, presumptive income is deemed to be 50% of gross receipts. So your presumptive income = 50% of Rs 40,00,000 = Rs 20,00,000. You need not maintain books of accounts. You can declare higher income but not lower than 50%. For businesses (not professions), Section 44AD applies with a Rs 3 crore limit (if digital receipts are 95%+) and 8% rate for cash / 6% rate for digital receipts. Under presumptive taxation, advance tax can be paid in a single instalment by March 15.
source: Sections 44AD, 44ADA, Income Tax Act (as amended by Finance Act 2023)

question_en: My father is 72 years old with pension income of Rs 5 lakh and FD interest of Rs 3 lakh. What are the special tax benefits available to him for FY 2025-26?
question_hi: मेरे पिता 72 वर्ष के हैं, पेंशन आय 5 लाख और FD ब्याज 3 लाख है। FY 2025-26 में उन्हें कौन से विशेष कर लाभ मिलते हैं?
question_hinglish: Mere father ki age 72 hai, pension income 5 lakh aur FD interest 3 lakh hai. FY 2025-26 mein unhe kaunse special tax benefits milte hain?
question_te: నా father కి 72 ఏళ్లు, pension income Rs 5 లక్షలు, FD interest Rs 3 లక్షలు. FY 2025-26 లో వారికి ఏ special tax benefits available?
question_bn: আমার বাবার বয়স 72, pension income Rs 5 লক্ষ আর FD interest Rs 3 লক্ষ। FY 2025-26-তে তাঁর জন্য কী কী special tax benefits আছে?
question_ta: என் அப்பாவுக்கு வயசு 72, pension income Rs 5 லட்சம், FD interest Rs 3 லட்சம். FY 2025-26-ல அவருக்கு என்னென்ன special tax benefits கிடைக்கும்?
answer: Senior citizens (60-80 years) get several benefits for FY 2025-26: (1) Under old regime, basic exemption limit is Rs 3 lakh (vs Rs 2.5 lakh for others). Under new regime, it is Rs 4 lakh for all. (2) Section 80TTB: Deduction up to Rs 50,000 on interest from deposits (savings, FD, post office) - available only under old regime. (3) No advance tax requirement: Senior citizens without business/professional income are exempt from paying advance tax under Section 207. Your father with only pension and FD interest need NOT pay advance tax. (4) Section 80D: Higher health insurance deduction limit of Rs 50,000 (vs Rs 25,000 for non-seniors). (5) Form 15H: Can submit to bank to avoid TDS on interest if total tax liability is nil. (6) ITR filing: Simplified ITR-1 can be used. For super senior citizens (80+), old regime exemption is Rs 5 lakh.

question_en: I won Rs 10 lakh in a TV game show and also earned Rs 2 lakh interest from fixed deposits. How are these taxed for FY 2025-26?
question_hi: मैंने एक TV गेम शो में 10 लाख रुपये जीते और फिक्स्ड डिपॉजिट से 2 लाख रुपये ब्याज भी कमाया। FY 2025-26 में इन पर कैसे कर लगेगा?
question_hinglish: Maine TV game show mein 10 lakh jeete aur FD se 2 lakh interest bhi earn kiya. FY 2025-26 mein yeh kaise tax hoga?
question_te: నేను TV game show లో Rs 10 లక్షలు గెలిచాను, FD నుండి Rs 2 లక్షల interest కూడా earn చేశాను. FY 2025-26 లో ఇవి ఎలా tax అవుతాయి?
question_bn: আমি TV game show-তে Rs 10 লক্ষ জিতেছি আর FD থেকে Rs 2 লক্ষ interest earned করেছি। FY 2025-26-তে এগুলো কীভাবে tax হবে?
question_ta: நான் TV game show-ல Rs 10 லட்சம் வென்றேன், FD-லிருந்து Rs 2 லட்சம் interest-ம் earn பண்ணினேன். FY 2025-26-ல இவை எப்படி tax ஆகும்?
answer: These are taxed very differently: (1) Game show winnings: Taxed at a flat 30% under Section 115BB (Income from Other Sources) regardless of your slab. No deductions or exemptions allowed. Tax = 30% of Rs 10,00,000 = Rs 3,00,000 plus 4% cess = Rs 3,12,000. TDS at 30% is deducted under Section 194B before payout. The Rs 12 lakh rebate under Section 87A does NOT apply to income taxed at special rates like winnings. (2) FD interest: Taxed at your normal slab rate under 'Income from Other Sources'. TDS is deducted by the bank at 10% under Section 194A if interest exceeds Rs 50,000 per year (Rs 1,00,000 for senior citizens, as revised by Budget 2025; earlier limits were Rs 40,000 and Rs 50,000 respectively). This income qualifies for normal slab rate taxation and deductions under old regime.

question_en: I sold my residential house for Rs 80 lakh with a long-term capital gain of Rs 25 lakh. I plan to buy a new house for Rs 60 lakh. Can I claim exemption under Section 54 for FY 2025-26?
question_hi: मैंने अपना मकान 80 लाख रुपये में बेचा, जिस पर 25 लाख का दीर्घकालिक पूंजी लाभ हुआ। मैं 60 लाख में नया मकान खरीदने की योजना बना रहा हूं। क्या मैं FY 2025-26 में धारा 54 के तहत छूट ले सकता हूं?
question_hinglish: Maine apna ghar 80 lakh mein becha, 25 lakh ka LTCG hua. 60 lakh ka naya ghar kharidna chahta hoon. Kya Section 54 mein exemption mil sakta hai FY 2025-26 mein?
question_te: నేను నా residential house Rs 80 లక్షలకు అమ్మాను, Rs 25 లక్షల LTCG వచ్చింది. Rs 60 లక్షలకు new house కొనాలనుకుంటున్నాను. FY 2025-26 లో Section 54 కింద exemption claim చేయగలనా?
question_bn: আমি আমার residential house Rs 80 লক্ষে বিক্রি করেছি, Rs 25 লক্ষ LTCG হয়েছে। Rs 60 লক্ষে নতুন বাড়ি কিনতে চাই। FY 2025-26-তে Section 54-এ exemption claim করতে পারব?
question_ta: நான் என் residential house-ஐ Rs 80 லட்சத்துக்கு விற்றேன், Rs 25 லட்சம் LTCG வந்தது. Rs 60 லட்சத்துக்கு புது house வாங்க plan பண்றேன். FY 2025-26-ல Section 54-ல exemption claim பண்ண முடியுமா?
answer: Yes, you can claim exemption under Section 54. Since your LTCG is Rs 25 lakh and you are reinvesting Rs 60 lakh (which exceeds the capital gain), the ENTIRE Rs 25 lakh LTCG is exempt. Key conditions: (1) The new house must be purchased within 2 years after sale or 1 year before sale, or constructed within 3 years; (2) Budget 2023 capped the maximum exemption under Section 54 at Rs 10 crore; (3) Only ONE new residential house can be purchased (the option to buy two houses for gains up to Rs 2 crore was a one-time benefit); (4) The new house cannot be sold within 3 years, else the exemption is reversed; (5) If the new house is not bought before ITR filing date, deposit the capital gain amount in Capital Gains Account Scheme (CGAS) in a bank. The exemption amount is the lower of LTCG (Rs 25 lakh) or cost of new house (Rs 60 lakh) = Rs 25 lakh.
source: Section 54, Income Tax Act (as amended by Finance Act 2023)

question_en: I want to send Rs 12 lakh abroad under LRS for my child's education. What TCS (Tax Collected at Source) will apply for FY 2025-26?
question_hi: मैं अपने बच्चे की शिक्षा के लिए LRS के तहत विदेश में 12 लाख रुपये भेजना चाहता हूं। FY 2025-26 में कितना TCS लगेगा?
question_hinglish: Main apne bachche ki education ke liye LRS ke through 12 lakh bahar bhejna chahta hoon. FY 2025-26 mein kitna TCS lagega?
question_te: నేను నా child education కోసం LRS కింద Rs 12 లక్షలు abroad పంపాలనుకుంటున్నాను. FY 2025-26 లో ఎంత TCS వస్తుంది?
question_bn: আমি আমার সন্তানের পড়াশোনার জন্য LRS-এর মাধ্যমে Rs 12 লক্ষ বিদেশে পাঠাতে চাই। FY 2025-26-তে কত TCS লাগবে?
question_ta: நான் என் child education-க்காக LRS-ல Rs 12 லட்சம் abroad அனுப்ப விரும்புகிறேன். FY 2025-26-ல எவ்வளவு TCS வரும்?
answer: Under Section 206C(1G), TCS on foreign remittance under LRS depends on the purpose and amount. Budget 2025 revised the thresholds effective FY 2025-26: No TCS on the first Rs 10 lakh of remittance per year (raised from Rs 7 lakh). Above Rs 10 lakh: (1) For overseas education funded by a loan from a financial institution: TCS has been removed entirely by Budget 2025; (2) For overseas education NOT funded by a loan: TCS is 5% on amounts above Rs 10 lakh; (3) For medical treatment: TCS is 5% above Rs 10 lakh; (4) For overseas tour packages: TCS is 20% on the entire amount (no threshold exemption) from October 2023; (5) For all other purposes (investment, gifts, etc.): TCS is 20% on amounts above Rs 10 lakh. For your case (Rs 12 lakh for education, assuming no loan): TCS = 5% of (Rs 12,00,000 - Rs 10,00,000) = 5% of Rs 2,00,000 = Rs 10,000. This TCS is adjustable against your income tax liability when filing ITR. The LRS limit is USD 2,50,000 per financial year per person.
source: Section 206C(1G), Income Tax Act (as amended by Finance Act 2023)

question_en: My employer granted me ESOPs. I exercised 500 options at an exercise price of Rs 100 when the fair market value was Rs 500 per share. Later I sold them at Rs 700 after 18 months. How is this taxed in FY 2025-26?
question_hi: मेरे नियोक्ता ने मुझे ESOP दिए। मैंने 500 ऑप्शन Rs 100 के एक्सरसाइज प्राइस पर एक्सरसाइज किए जब FMV Rs 500 प्रति शेयर था। बाद में 18 महीने बाद Rs 700 में बेचा। FY 2025-26 में इस पर कैसे कर लगेगा?
question_hinglish: Meri company ne ESOP diye. Maine 500 options exercise kiye Rs 100 pe jab FMV Rs 500 tha. Phir 18 months baad Rs 700 pe sell kiya. FY 2025-26 mein tax kaise lagega?
question_te: నా employer నాకు ESOPs ఇచ్చారు. Exercise price Rs 100 తో FMV Rs 500 ఉన్నప్పుడు 500 options exercise చేశాను. 18 months తర్వాత Rs 700 కి sell చేశాను. FY 2025-26 లో tax ఎలా వస్తుంది?
question_bn: আমার employer ESOP দিয়েছে। Exercise price Rs 100-তে 500 options exercise করেছি যখন FMV ছিল Rs 500। 18 মাস পরে Rs 700-তে বিক্রি করেছি। FY 2025-26-তে tax কীভাবে হবে?
question_ta: என் employer ESOP கொடுத்தார். Exercise price Rs 100-ல FMV Rs 500 இருக்கும்போது 500 options exercise பண்ணினேன். 18 months கழிச்சு Rs 700-க்கு sell பண்ணினேன். FY 2025-26-ல tax எப்படி வரும்?
answer: ESOP taxation in India has two stages: Stage 1 - At Exercise (Perquisite tax): The difference between FMV on exercise date and exercise price is taxed as salary perquisite. Perquisite = (Rs 500 - Rs 100) x 500 shares = Rs 2,00,000. This is added to your salary and taxed at your slab rate. Employer deducts TDS on this. For eligible startups under Section 80-IAC, tax on perquisite can be deferred up to 5 years or until sale/leaving the company (whichever is earlier). Stage 2 - At Sale (Capital gains): The difference between sale price and FMV on exercise date is capital gain. Gain = (Rs 700 - Rs 500) x 500 = Rs 1,00,000. Since held for more than 12 months after exercise, this is LTCG. Under Section 112A: LTCG on listed shares above Rs 1.25 lakh is taxed at 12.5%. Since Rs 1,00,000 is below the Rs 1.25 lakh exemption, capital gains tax = NIL. Total tax: Slab rate on Rs 2,00,000 perquisite + Nil on capital gains.
source: Section 17(2)(vi), Section 112A, Section 80-IAC, Income Tax Act

question_en: My total taxable income is Rs 55 lakh for FY 2025-26 under the new regime. How do I calculate the surcharge and cess on my income tax?
question_hi: FY 2025-26 में नई व्यवस्था के तहत मेरी कुल कर योग्य आय 55 लाख रुपये है। मैं अपने आयकर पर सरचार्ज और सेस की गणना कैसे करूं?
question_hinglish: FY 2025-26 mein new regime ke under meri total taxable income 55 lakh hai. Surcharge aur cess kaise calculate karun apne income tax pe?
question_te: FY 2025-26 లో new regime కింద నా total taxable income Rs 55 లక్షలు. నా income tax మీద surcharge మరియు cess ఎలా calculate చేయాలి?
question_bn: FY 2025-26-তে new regime-এ আমার total taxable income Rs 55 লক্ষ। আমার income tax-এর ওপর surcharge আর cess কীভাবে calculate করব?
question_ta: FY 2025-26-ல new regime-ல என் total taxable income Rs 55 லட்சம். என் income tax மேல surcharge மற்றும் cess எப்படி calculate பண்றது?
answer: Step-by-step calculation under the new regime (Budget 2025 slabs) for Rs 55 lakh income: (1) Tax calculation: Rs 0-4L = Nil; Rs 4-8L = 5% of 4L = Rs 20,000; Rs 8-12L = 10% of 4L = Rs 40,000; Rs 12-16L = 15% of 4L = Rs 60,000; Rs 16-20L = 20% of 4L = Rs 80,000; Rs 20-24L = 25% of 4L = Rs 1,00,000; Rs 24-55L = 30% of 31L = Rs 9,30,000. Total tax = Rs 16,30,000. (2) Surcharge: For income Rs 50L-1 crore, surcharge is 10% of tax. Surcharge = 10% of Rs 16,30,000 = Rs 1,63,000. But marginal relief applies: total tax+surcharge on Rs 55L should not exceed total tax on Rs 50L + income exceeding Rs 50L. Tax on Rs 50L = Rs 14,80,000. So max tax+surcharge = Rs 14,80,000 + Rs 5,00,000 = Rs 19,80,000. Actual tax+surcharge = Rs 16,30,000 + Rs 1,63,000 = Rs 17,93,000. Since Rs 17,93,000 < Rs 19,80,000, no marginal relief needed. (3) Health & Education Cess = 4% of (tax + surcharge) = 4% of Rs 17,93,000 = Rs 71,720. Total tax liability = Rs 18,64,720. Surcharge rates under new regime: 10% (Rs 50L-1Cr), 15% (Rs 1Cr-2Cr), 25% (above Rs 2Cr, capped at 25% for new regime).
